vancode/interpreter/sym

        unwrapType
      • unwrapType(ty: Sym): Sym

Implements the symbol and scope system for the interpreter. This is used to manage variables, functions, types, and other symbols in the interpreter. It also handles symbol lookup, overloading, and type checking.

Imports

ast, value

Types

ArgTuple = tuple[sym: Sym, exprSym: Sym]
CodeGenError = object of ValueError
  file*: string
  ln*, col*: int
Context = distinct uint16
A scope context. 
Module {.acyclic.} = ref object of Scope
  name*: string              ## the name of the module
  src*: Option[string]       ## the source file where the module was defined
                             ## this is used for type checking and error reporting
  modules*: Table[string, Module] ## a table of modules imported by this module
                                  ## where the key is the path to the module
A module representing the global scope of a single source file. 
ObjectField = tuple[id: int, name: Node, ty: Sym, implVal: Sym]
ProcParam = tuple[name: Node, ty: Sym, implSym: Sym, isMut: bool, isOpt: bool]
A single param of a proc. 
ProcType = enum
  procTypeFunction, procTypeMacro
The type of a procedure (function) 
Scope {.acyclic.} = ref object of RootObj
  syms*: Table[string, Sym]
  exportSyms*: Table[string, Sym] ## A table of exported symbols
  otherSyms*: Table[string, Sym] ## A table of symbols imported from other modules
  variables*, exportVariables*: Table[string, Sym] ## A table of variables. This is used for fast lookups of variables
                                                   ## in the current scope. The key is the hash of the variable's name.
  functions*, exportFunctions*: Table[string, Sym] ## A table of functions. This is used for fast lookups of functions
                                                   ## in the current scope.
  typeDefs*, exportTypeDefs*: Table[string, Sym] ## A table of type definitions. This is used for fast lookups of type
                                                 ## definitions in the current scope.
  context*: Context          ## the scope's context. this is used for scope hygiene
A local scope. 
Sym {.acyclic.} = ref object
  name*: Node                ## the name of the symbol
  impl*: Node                ## the implementation of the symbol. may be ``nil`` if the symbol is generated
  src*: Option[string]       ## the source file where the symbol was defined
                             ## this is used for type checking and error reporting
  case kind*: SymKind
  of skVar, skLet, skConst:
    varTy*: Sym              ## the type of the variable
    varSet*: bool            ## is the variable set?
    varLocal*: bool          ## is the variable local?
    varStackPos*: int        ## the position of this local variable on the stack
    varExport*: bool         ## whether the variable is exported or not
  of skType:
    typeExport*: bool        ## This is used to determine whether the type
                             ## can be used in other modules or not.
    case tyKind*: TypeKind
    of ttyNil:
      nil
    of ttyVoid .. ttyString:
      isMutable*: bool
    of ttyJson:
      jsonTy*: JsonNodeKind  ## the type of the JSON value, used for type checking
      jsonNode*: JsonNode    ## a statically defined JSON node (when available)
    of ttyArray:
      arrayTy*: Sym          ## the type of the array
      arrayMutable*: bool    ## used to determine whether the array is mutable or not
      arrayItems*: seq[Sym]  ## the items of the array
    of ttyObject:
      objectId*: TypeId      ## a unique id for this object type, used for type checking and comparisons
      objectFields*: OrderedTable[string, ObjectField]
    of ttyAlias:
      aliasId*: TypeId       ## the id of the alias, used for lookups and type checking
      aliasTy*: Sym          ## the type this alias refers to
    of ttyCustom:
      nil
    of ttyHtmlElement:
      nil
    of ttyPointer:
      pointerTarget*: Sym    ## The type this pointer points to (type info only)
    else:
      nil
  of skHtmlType:
    tag*, innerText*: string ## the tag of the HTML element (e.g., "div", "span", etc.)
    isVoidElement*: bool     ## whether this HTML element is a void element or not (e.g., "img", "br", etc.)
  of skProc:
    procId*: uint16          ## the unique number of the proc
    sourcePath*: string      ## the source path of the proc
    procParams*: seq[ProcParam] ## the proc's parameters
    procReturnTy*: Sym       ## the return type of the proc
    procType*: ProcType      ## whether the proc is a normal function or a macro
    procExport*: bool        ## whether the proc is exported or not
    procMemoize*: bool       ## whether the proc is memoized or not. todo: implement memoization
  of skIterator:
    iterParams*: seq[ProcParam] ## the iterator's parameters
    iterYieldTy*: Sym        ## the yield type of the iterator
    iterExport*: bool        ## whether the iterator is exported or not
  of skGenericParam:
    constraint*: Sym         ## the generic type constraint
  of skChoice:
    choices*: seq[Sym]       ## the different choices for this overloaded symbol
  genericParams*: Option[seq[Sym]] ## some if the sym is generic
  genericInstCache*: Table[seq[Sym], Sym]
  genericBase*: Option[Sym]  ## contains the base generic type if the sym is an instantiation
  genericInstArgs*: Option[seq[Sym]] ## some if the sym is an instantiation
A symbol. This represents an ident that can be looked up. 
SymKind = enum
  skVar = "var", skConst = "const", skLet = "let", skType = "type",
  skProc = "fn", skIterator = "iterator", skGenericParam = "generic param",
  skHtmlType = "html", skChoice = "(...)" ## an overloaded symbol, stores many symbols with \
                                          ## the same name
The kind of a symbol. 
TypeKind {..} = enum
  ttyVoid = "void", ttyAny = "any", ttyBool = "bool", ttyInt = "int",
  ttyFloat = "float", ttyString = "string", ttyPointer = "pointer",
  ttyProc = "proc", ttyJson = "json", ttyArray = "array", ttyNil = "nil",
  ttyObject = "object", ttyAlias = "alias", ttyCustom = "custom",
  ttyHtmlElement = "HtmlElement"
The kind of a type.

Consts

skCallable = {skProc, skIterator}
skDecl = {skVar..skIterator}
skTyped = {skType, skHtmlType, skGenericParam}
skVars = {skVar, skLet, skConst}
tyMeta = {ttyVoid, ttyAny}
tyPrimitives = {ttyVoid..ttyString}

Procs

proc `$`(module: Module): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Stringifies a module. This is only really useful for debugging. 
proc `$`(params: seq[ProcParam]): string {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Stringify a seq of proc parameters. 
proc `$`(scope: Scope): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Stringifies a scope. This is only really useful for debugging. 
proc `$`(sym: Sym): string {....raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect],
                             forbids: [].}
proc `==`(a, b: Context): bool {.borrow, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc add(scope: Scope; sym: Sym; lookupName: Node = nil;
         fromOtherModule: static bool = false): bool {.discardable.}
Add a symbol to the given scope. If a symbol under the given name already exists, it's added into an skChoice. The rules for overloading are:
  • there may only be one skVar or skLet under a given skChoice,
  • there may only be one skType under a given skChoice,
  • there may be any number of skProcs with unique parameters under a single skChoice.

If any one of these checks fails, the proc will return false. These checks will probably made more strict in the future.

proc addCallable(scope: Scope; sym: Sym; lookupName: Node;
                 fromOtherModule: static bool = false): bool {.discardable.}
Add a callable to the given scope. If sym is itself an overload choice, add each overload individually. 
proc addType(scope: Scope; sym: Sym; lookupName: Node;
             fromOtherModule: static bool = false): bool {.discardable.}
Add a type to the given scope. 
proc addVariable(scope: Scope; sym: Sym; lookupName: Node;
                 fromOtherModule: static bool = false): bool {.discardable.}
Add a variable to the given scope. 
proc canAdd(choice, sym: Sym): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Tests if sym can be added into choice. Refer to add documentation below for details. 
proc clone(sym: Sym): Sym {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Clones a symbol, returning a newly allocated instance with the same fields. 
proc genPtr(module: Module; typeId: TypeId; name: string): Sym {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc genType(kind: TypeKind; name: string; exportSym: bool;
             genericParams: Option[seq[Sym]] = none(seq[Sym])): Sym {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Generate a new type symbol from a string name. 
proc getModuleName(module: Module): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Get the module name for the JS export This proc will transform the file name into a valid JS identifier 
proc hash(sym: Sym): Hash {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Hashes a sym (for use in Tables). 
proc initSystemTypes(module: Module) {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Add primitive types into the module. This should only ever be called when creating the system module. 
proc isGeneric(sym: Sym): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns whether the symbol is generic or not. A symbol is generic if it has generic params or is a generic param. 
proc isInstantiation(sym: Sym): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns whether the symbol is an instantiation. 
proc load(module: Module; other: Module; fromOtherModule: static bool = false): bool {.
    discardable.}
Import public symbols from other into module If the module is already imported it will return false 
proc newModule(name: string; src: Option[string] = none(string)): Module {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Initialize a new module. 
proc newSym(kind: SymKind; name: Node; impl: Node = nil): Sym {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create a new symbol from a Node. 
proc newType(kind: TypeKind; name: Node; impl: Node = nil): Sym {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create a new type symbol from a Node. 
proc params(sym: Sym): seq[ProcParam] {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Get the proc/iterator's params. 
proc returnTy(sym: Sym): Sym {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Get the proc/iterator's return or yield type. 
proc sameParams(a, b: Sym): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Overload of sameParams for two symbols. 
proc sameParams(sym: Sym; args: seq[Sym]): bool {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Returns true if both a and b are called with the same parameters. 
proc sameType(a, b: Sym): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Returns true if a and b are compatible types. 
proc sym(module: Module; name: string): Sym {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Get the symbol name from a module. 
proc unwrapType(ty: Sym): Sym {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Unwraps a type from any skVar or skLet wrappers.

Made with Nim. Generated: 2026-07-11 08:55:12 UTC
